nodes.title: SEGS Prompts
widgets_values: Use these Positive Prompt windows to give detailed instructions to the detailer! When you're not sure what to tell it, simply prompt "hand" (or "face" or whatever the BBox selection you are using) and the model will try to figure it out on a case-by-case basis. Consider turning off the detailer whenever the generations are clean.

nodes.title: Size Adjust
widgets_values: Although I don't recommend going BIG, sometimes for testing purposes you may wish to adjust or shrink the image from the preset SDXL recommended resolutions, and this allows you to do so. But generally don't mess with the standard resolutions!

nodes.title: Image Saver and Bypass Information
widgets_values:
This workflow features **3 Image Saver Nodes:** 2 within the Spaghetti Region and 1 within the Upscale Group. Edit these nodes to change File Name, Directory etc. Metadata and Civit Download Options are found in the Green Section of Input/Output.

Do not Bypass the Bypass Core!

This logic was implemented to save files only when necessary. Here is the save concept in a nutshell: You can toggle 1st pass from the inputs toggle. If it's on, your first pass image gets saved. If you use HiresFix, that gets saved on the 1st pass instead of your original low resolution generation. If you use Upscaler, the Upscaled image gets saved on the 2nd pass. If you use the detailer, the final pass save is saved as the 3rd pass. Files are not written until the entire generation is complete. The Error Problem: If you disable the detailers, the Core will not send an image to the final pass image saver, causing an error, which can be ignored, but it's annoying!! ComfyUI-Image-Saver really should update the code so that it fails gracefully! If this error bothers you, you can edit the nodes.py file if you're brave **or just bypass the final pass image saver node** but remember to turn it back on when you are using the detailers.

nodes.title: Detailer LoRAs
widgets_values:
Blocking Other LoRAs

It is possible to have Detailer LoRAs act upon the detailer and also block other LoRAs from acting upon the detailer by enabling the LoRA Blocker node. You may wish to do this if you find your LoRAs are turning hands into macaroni alfredo or faces are coming out wrong. If you find that your LoRA set is interfering in some way, enable the blocker, which will put the checkpoint base model at the top of the switching priority, and it will be used. Thanks to Lonecatone23 for helping me out with this switching method!

nodes.title: Detailer Note and Guidance
widgets_values:
If you are struggling with understanding how to use the detailers, please note I have a guide to this detailer here: https://civitai.com/articles/26885/using-the-bbox-sorter-mgherders-detailer-array

Fow now, there are no LoRA recommendations for Anima detailers.

nodes.title: SEGS Detailer Array
widgets_values:
The SEGS Detailer lets you choose how to fill in critical details using BBOX detectors.

The VRAM has to be cleaned between Detailer passes to prevent bleed-over artifacts, particularly if the bbox is large. BBox detection goes from left to right by default and top to bottom. Note that there will be outputs showing the bbox preview but if a bbox is not found, a preview from the previous successful detection will still remain in the preview box - in other words, it will look like it is detecting, but it is not. One telltale sign of this is if the bboxes are out of order.

Once you are satisfied with the generated results you can fix the seed and work on details.

"Work on Details" means using the prompts and the Primitives to home in on a better result. For instance, adjusting facial expressions and eye color. Feel free to use natural language.

A detailer LoRA is provided but you may need to configure it yourself. Since Anima has so few detailer LoRAs you may find no use for this LoRA, so leave it on bypass and leave it outside of the detailer array or it will be turned off and on with the fast bypasser, causing unwanted results. When you get a detailer you want to use, you should integrate it accordingly.

**Remember, detailers use denoise settings** and these can make a big difference.

The detector will not always detect what is there. Lowering the threshold of the BBOX Detector makes detection easier, while raising it can help filter out false positives. Default is 0.5.

For a more detailed guide on how to use these detailer arrays, see the link below:

https://civitai.com/articles/26885/using-the-bbox-sorter-mgherders-detailer-array

nodes.title: Upscale and HiresFix
widgets_values:
If you are using
Detailers and HiresFix
it is strongly recommended to Disable Upscale. You only need either HiresFix or Upscale, not both, but the choice to use both is up to you.

nodes.title: Upscaler 1.2
widgets_values:
# Upscaler Nodes

The Upscaler is triggered before the detailer, and is designed to hopefully increase the overall quality of the image without introducing hallucinations. If you get hallucinations such as exaggerated body features or whatever, decrease denoise (to around .18) as this should clamp down on the original image.

I tried to incorporate the Lightning LoRA here but it did not improve generation speed. However, I did find almost the same results in overall generation with just 8 steps instead of 30+ steps.

The upscaler's seed is tied to the main generation.

A color matching step is included in Upscaler 1.2 which negates the way that some checkpoints undergo a slight yellowing during upscaling.

nodes.title: NEW
widgets_values:
NEW WITH v5.2

Bypasser Logic 1.0:

This system saves the Final Pass image only when the detailers are activated, saving you hard drive space without worrying about if you remembered to turn them on or off or messing with the Bypass Upscaler toggle. That stupid thing is finally gone. However, the tradeoff is an error if you are not using detailers. Best I can do for now.

Efficiency Nodes Preview KSampler Out

I decided to straight up copy Lonecat's method of refiner pass. Sorry it kind of bugs me too having to wait to see what's going on in there. It's not necessary to do a refiner pass but I have been liking the results and honestly efficiency nodes is giving me problems updating so that's worrisome. The Preview KSampler is still a part of the SDXL workflow.

nodes.title: QuickStart Guide
widgets_values:
Anima TEXT2IMG

This is a t2i workflow. It has 3 metadata-inclusive save nodes: For the First Pass, after the Detailer, and after Upscaler.

Order of Operations

0) Configure Generation Settings 1) Image Generation with Preview 2) Hires Fix 3) Save First Pass 4) Upscale, Color Match & Save 5) Hand Detailer 6) Face Detailer 7) If Detailers used, Final Save Use Style Presets to switch between standard Quality tags for Anime or Realism, and make other prompts or use none if you prefer. **Disclaimer: This will not force the checkpoint to follow the style you want. LoRAs and the checkpoint itself can override stylistic instructions.** The purpose is to quickly change presets when you change checkpoints. You usually don't need both HiresFix and Upscaler. If you do use both, HiresFix is likely to significantly slow down Upscaler. If both Upscale and Hires Fix is skipped, detailer results are unlikely to improve smaller bboxes.

For Speed:

2 Options: Use HiresFix *or* Skip Hires and Detailer, Use Upscaler Only Alternatively, if you want to use the Detailer but don't want to wait for the Upscaler, just turn off upscaler, and your HiresFix version of the image will be what is sent to the detailer, automagically. If you skip the detailer, you may want to set the final pass save node to bypass to avoid the error.

For Quality:

When you *really* have an image you like, *Freeze the seed,* and enable HiresFix AND/OR Upscaler and crank it up to 1.5 (upscale) or 50% (HiresFix) or so. Heck you could go even higher if you have the VRAM, but that's enough for me most times. If you enable the Detailer, check the prompts and run a pass. Check the SEGS previews and adjust the prompts accordingly.
